title: ‘Closed’ expressway, birthday bash, body parts scattered: How a BMW ride turned fatal for 2
content: A birthday celebration in Badlapur ended in tragedy as a speeding BMW crashed on the underconstruction Mumbai-Delhi Expressway, killing two young individuals and severely injuring the driver. The car was reportedly travelling at extreme speeds, with a social media post suggesting up to 251kmph. Investigators suspect reckless driving and potential attempts to avoid obstructions led to the devastating accident, leaving the vehicle mangled and parts scattered.

title: Congress vs Congress again: Tharoor faces fresh backlash over Kashmir remarks
content: Congress leader Shashi Tharoor faced internal party criticism for his remarks on Kashmir's progress towards normalcy and for praising PM Modi's stance on civilian sailors' safety. Critics questioned his engagement with party members and ground realities in Kashmir, while another party leader sarcastically questioned his interpretation of Modi's statements. Tharoor defended his comments, citing widely published reports and emphasizing concern for Indian lives.

title: NEET re-exam 2026: Tight security, dress debates & a 'tough' paper— how the day unfolded
content: Millions of medical aspirants reappeared for the NEET-UG 2026 re-exam nationwide after the original test was cancelled due to paper leak allegations. Unprecedented security measures, including biometrics and CCTV, were implemented. While students reported a moderate-to-tough paper, particularly Physics, the day also saw emotional moments and entry disputes. The focus now shifts to answer keys, results, and the ongoing CBI investigation.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
content: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

title: 'Have made great progress': Vance hails talks with Iran while Trump issues threats
content: US Vice President JD Vance engaged in high-level meetings with Iranian officials in Switzerland, marking a crucial step towards solidifying a recent interim agreement. The key discussions revolved around encouraging Iran's adherence to nuclear talks and pursuing a comprehensive ceasefire in the region, notably addressing the escalating conflict in Lebanon. Iran reiterated its focus on prioritizing regional security amid these vital negotiations.

title: UK telecom giants roll out 'kill switch' to block stolen phones from working: How it works
content: UK mobile giants Virgin Media O2 and VodafoneThree have activated a new 'kill switch' for new phones stolen from their stores, aiming to cripple the black market for stolen devices. This move follows resistance from major manufacturers like Apple and Samsung to a universal anti-theft lock. The technology renders stolen, unsold handsets unusable remotely, a measure previously seen in the Netherlands.

title: SRS 2024: 3.5% people were 'widowed, divorced or separated' with 5.5% women and 1.6% men
content: Latest data reveals a significant gender disparity in marital status, with 3.5% of the population widowed, divorced, or separated in 2024. Women disproportionately represent this group at 5.4% compared to men's 1.6%, highlighting unique challenges. Southern states, particularly Tamil Nadu, show the highest percentages for both the general population and women in this category.

title: Anthropic co-founder who said software engineering is 'dead', now says days of AI prompts are over
content: AI pioneer Boris Cherny, co-founder of Anthropic, is shifting his focus from manual prompt writing to 'loop engineering.' This new approach involves AI agents autonomously generating and refining prompts, moving beyond constant human input.  Other AI leaders like Peter Steinberger and Addy Osmani echo this sentiment, emphasizing the design of these AI 'loops' over direct prompting. This evolution positions AI agents as more akin to employees, managing tasks and workflows.

title: Heat dome over Europe brings travel chaos, health alerts and wildlife stress
content: Europe is grappling with a severe heatwave, with temperatures soaring towards 40°C, triggering health alerts and transport chaos. Italy has issued red alerts for eight cities, while France faces significant rail disruptions. Wildlife is also under immense pressure. Experts link these extreme conditions to climate change, with concerns growing over prolonged heat and its impact on public safety and infrastructure across the continent.

title: Fortnite x Olivia Rodrigo confirmed: Skins, release date, price details & more
content: Pop sensation Olivia Rodrigo is set to make a grand entrance into Fortnite, with her collaboration officially confirmed.  Fans caught a glimpse of her upcoming Icon Series skin, inspired by her latest album, through a social media story.  The highly anticipated cosmetic bundle, featuring an outfit, back bling, and more, is expected to launch with Fortnite Festival Season 15 around July 29, 2026, likely priced around 2,500 V-Bucks.

title: Brazil fan tries Rocky Balboa ‘curse’ to derail Lionel Messi’s World Cup dream
content: A Brazil fan sparked online buzz after placing Lionel Messi's Argentina jersey on Philadelphia's Rocky Balboa statue, a tradition believed by some rival supporters to jinx opponents. The stunt came as Argentina prepares to face Austria after Messi's historic hat trick against Algeria. Meanwhile, Brazil climbed to the top of Group C with a 3-0 win over Haiti and will next face Scotland in the FIFA World Cup.

title: Taylor Swift and Blake Lively’s friendship faces strain ahead of wedding
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have maintained privacy even though they are pretty public about their high profile relationship for the last three years. While both of them have remained publicly distant from Blake Lively, it seems Taylor Swift’s friendship with her had taken a dramatic turn, just a few weeks ago as the pop star now gears up to finally get married.

title: Marizanne Kapp’s unbeaten 81 sinks India as South Africa secure six-wicket win
content: Marizanne Kapp's stellar all-round display secured a six-wicket victory for South Africa against India in the Women's T20 World Cup. After taking two crucial wickets, Kapp smashed an unbeaten 81 off 45 balls, including seven fours and four sixes. Despite India posting 158/7, dropped catches proved costly as Kapp guided South Africa to a thrilling chase with five balls to spare.

title: 'Robust system': CBSE declares over 87% reval results as marking controversy persists
content: The CBSE has begun releasing Class 12 verification and re-evaluation results, covering over 87% of applications. This follows student concerns about potential answer sheet mismatches with their handwriting, raising transparency issues. The board assures a robust system and urges students to rely on official communications, with remaining results to be announced soon via DigiLocker.

title: Happy Father’s Day 2026: Top 50 wishes, messages and quotes to make dad feel special
content: Fathers often express love through actions, not words, and Father's Day on June 21, 2026, is a time to appreciate their guidance and support. Simple gestures like cooking a favorite meal or sharing memories can mean more than gifts. This day is about acknowledging their sacrifices and letting them know they are deeply appreciated.

title: How China is using maritime tactics and propaganda to squeeze Taiwan
content: Taiwan's security official warns China is increasingly using 'grey-zone' tactics like coast guard patrols and legal claims to pressure the island, moving away from direct military threats. Beijing is exploiting international law and maritime activities to advance its objectives, while Taiwan is urged to adopt 'absolute transparency' and enhance regional cooperation to counter these evolving challenges amidst ongoing Chinese military drills.

title: Can daily yoga help maintain testosterone naturally? What doctors want men to know
content: Yoga may offer a natural boost to testosterone levels by significantly reducing stress and improving sleep quality, both crucial for hormone production. While not a direct stimulant, its benefits in calming the nervous system, enhancing sleep, and aiding in fat reduction and insulin sensitivity contribute to overall hormonal balance. For many, it's a valuable addition to a healthy lifestyle for better endocrine function.

title: Matt Henry rewrites history, becomes first bowler since Shane Warne to...
content: Matt Henry delivered a career-defining performance at The Oval, claiming 11 wickets to help New Zealand level the Test series against England. His exceptional spell, including a devastating 6-29 in the second innings, etched his name in the venue's history, mirroring legendary feats.  Recovered from an injury-plagued first Test, Henry's resurgence showcased his prowess as New Zealand's leading pace bowler.

title: Former USSR leader's great-grandson captured by Ukraine: Reports
content: Reports indicate Anton Milaev, adopted great-grandson of Leonid Brezhnev, has been captured by Ukrainian forces. Milaev, reportedly a sapper, was last heard from in November 2025. Ukrainian military intelligence and prisoner of war coordination sources have allegedly confirmed his custody in the Kherson region. His presence on a Ukrainian 'enemies' database has also been noted. The circumstances of his capture and potential for exchange remain unclear amidst ongoing conflict.

title: Big jolt to Thackeray: Sena (UBT) MP joins Shinde, Fadnavis calls 'operation successful'
content: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis hinted at a significant political shift involving Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Omraje Nimbalkar, suggesting a successful "operation" and promising "breaking news" soon from Deputy CM Eknath Shinde. Nimbalkar met supporters to discuss his future, amid speculation he might join the Shinde-led Shiv Sena. This potential defection follows his father's acquittal in a murder case, which he plans to challenge in the high court.

title: People who thank others excessively may be surprised by support without strings attached
content: Over-thanking for small favors reveals a nuanced experience of connection and care, psychology suggests. It's not just politeness; it signals feeling truly seen and valued. This response can also stem from unease about owing a debt or social pressure to appear grateful. Such reactions highlight the significance of unconditional kindness, reflecting a deep appreciation for genuine human connection.

title: Death trap in the deep: The silent sinkhole of Bengaluru’s sewage plant
content: Two workers vanished inside a Bengaluru sewage plant, sparking outrage from activists who claim they were illegally hired for hazardous work without safety. The water authority denies manual scavenging, stating the men entered unauthorizedly while attempting gate repairs. The incident highlights ongoing concerns about worker safety and accountability in the city's sanitation sector, with calls for prosecution of responsible officials.

title: 'Stokes will be captain': McCullum drops massive update after 253-run loss
content: England captain Ben Stokes will return for the third Test against New Zealand despite an ongoing disciplinary investigation over a curfew breach. Coach Brendon McCullum confirmed Stokes will lead the side after impressing with Durham. Gus Atkinson is also in line for a comeback. McCullum dismissed talk of tensions, insisting there remains mutual respect between him and Stokes.

title: 'Modi destroying India, Trump the world': Kharge takes 'good friends' dig at PM
content: Blaming Trump for rising fuel prices, he criticised the Modi government's foreign policy, saying India had moved away from its long-standing non-aligned approach. "India followed a policy of non-alignment since Nehru's time and maintained good relations with all countries. But that policy has been abandoned, and Modi, by going around calling everyone his friend and hugging them, has brought us to this situation."

title: Joe Root equals unwanted record after England's crushing defeat to NZ
content: Joe Root's batting prowess shone at The Oval, but England's heavy defeat to New Zealand saw him equal Stephen Fleming's record for most Test captaincy losses with 27.  Despite a strong performance from Root, England's depleted side, featuring three debutants, struggled against a dominant New Zealand, who secured a commanding 253-run victory. Matt Henry's exceptional bowling proved decisive in the series-levelling win.

title: Map, hunt, kill: Inside India's powerful trishul for networked naval warfare
content: India's Navy is enhancing its maritime warfare capabilities by integrating new vessels into a cohesive network. INS Dunagiri, a stealth frigate, INS Sanshodhak, a survey ship, and INS Agray, an anti-submarine craft, were commissioned, showcasing a strategy focused on layered awareness and combat power. This integrated approach aims to bolster India's security in the increasingly active Indian Ocean.

title: 'Social media post shows BMW at 251 kmph': 2 killed in Delhi-Mumbai Expressway crash
content: A birthday celebration turned tragic on the under-construction Delhi-Mumbai Expressway near Badlapur, claiming two lives and injuring another after a speeding BMW crashed into a divider.  The luxury car was severely mangled, with its engine found meters away.  Investigators are examining a social media post allegedly showing the car at 251 kmph, suspecting reckless driving. The surviving driver's statement is pending.

title: Sooryavanshi's potential India debut gets green signal after Cricket Ireland's big decision
content: Cricket Ireland has confirmed the upcoming T20I series against India will proceed as planned in Belfast on June 26 and 28. Following a security review with local authorities, officials are confident in the safety measures for players and spectators. This marks India's return to Stormont after nearly two decades, with anticipation building around potential senior debut for young sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.

title: Nihangs storm Uttarakhand Gurdwara, hold manager hostage over arrests after Karnaprayag clash
content: Nihangs have taken a gurdwara manager hostage in Uttarakhand's Rudraprayag, demanding the release of four members arrested after a clash in Karnaprayag. The group, armed with traditional weapons, occupied the gurdwara roof, raising slogans. The incident stems from a dispute over parking that escalated into violence, leading to the arrests. Authorities are negotiating for their descent while investigating the initial clash.

title: ‘Fund crunch, caustic remarks’: Uddhav camp MP confirms crossover to ruling Sena
content: A Shiv Sena (UBT) Lok Sabha MP, Nagesh Patil Ashtikar, has switched to the Eknath Shinde faction, citing a need for development funds for his constituency. He stated that the limited MPLADS fund was insufficient to address public expectations. Ashtikar also alluded to "certain remarks" made by a senior Sena (UBT) leader as a catalyst for his decision, suggesting a lack of respect within the party.

title: Group of Nihangs storm gurdwara in Rudraprayag, hold Sikh devotee hostage on roof
content: A heavy police force was deployed at the site following the standoff that began on Saturday (June 20, 2026) evening and is still on, even though the district administration on Sunday (June 21, 2026) said the situation is fully under control and peaceful

title: End the free rein of junk food advertising in India
content: Restricting the advertising of ultra-processed food (UPF) products and foods high in fat, sugar and sodium (HFSS) is a public health imperative

title: JD(U) national council unanimously endorses Nitish Kumar as national president, calls Nishant its future
content: Nitish Kumar says the JD(U) has taken a place in the hearts of people by fighting for ‘development with justice’, a commitment that, according to him, has resulted in the party’s primary membership exceeding 10 million

title: Patients face pedestrian, civic challenges around major government hospitals in Bengaluru
content: Encroached footpaths, unsafe crossings, flooding and inadequate patient facilities continue to inconvenience visitors despite improvements in traffic management at some locations.

title: The lack of accountability within the NTA
content: The National Testing Agency (NTA) was created in 2017 as a registered society under the Societies Registration Act, 1860 and not through an Act of Parliament, which means it operates without a codified liability standard toward the candidates it examines

title: Cyber scam hubs across Southeast Asia fuelled by human trafficking: APG report
content: Government says 6,998 Indians have been rescued from cyber scam hubs since 2022—2,533 from Cambodia, 2,297 from Lao PDR, and 2,168 from Myanmar

title: Around 600 tonnes of fish sold at Kasimedu on Sunday
content: Fishermen proudly showed off large-sized grouper fish, also called Kalavaan, and sharks caught in their nets

title: Maharashtra CM accuses of MVA of sending AI-generated letter to boycott tea party
content: Mr. Fadnavis said that this letter was drafted on Open AI’s chatbot Chat GPT and mockingly said that he was happy the opposition was using technology

title: ‘Operation Toofan’: DANSAF, police intensify checks on inter-State trucks along highways
content: Special squads, including plainclothesmen and shadow patrol squads, have been deployed at strategic locations along national and State highways and at border transit points to conduct inspections

title: Preparations in full swing for 12th edition of Malabar River Festival
content: Kayakers from Kerala and other States are training in Iruvanjippuzha and Chalipuzha ahead of the festival; pre-event programmes are under way to encourage wider public participation

title: Political parties condole death in ammonia leak at seafood processing unit in Tiruvallur district
content: They urge the Tamil Nadu government to ensure safety guidelines are strictly followed in industrial units

title: CISF to be deployed at five more prisons in Jammu and Kashmir
content: This includes a new high-security prison in Kathua, four district jails; CISF implements tech upgrades at Central jails, including AI-enabled CCTV systems and advanced video analytics for real-time monitoring

title: Engineer’s body recovered from Varthur STP; search continues for another missing worker
content: Varthur Police have registered a FIR against the private firm contracted for maintenance, based on a complaint from BWSSB

title: Gunfire at Congress MLA’s office shows law and order collapse in Haryana, says Hooda
content: Not a single gang was active during the Congress government’s tenure, because the administration’s policy was clear: either give up crime or leave Haryana, says Leader of Opposition Bhupinder Singh Hooda

title: CBSE announces Class 12 re-valuation results
content: “The outcomes are being released in a phased manner, with over 87% of the total applications received being declared today. The outcomes of the remaining applications will be available in phases, and the entire process is expected to be completed soon,” says CBSE

title: Right to vote should be a Fundamental Right: Jairam Ramesh
content: This will ensure safeguards against “voter suppressions” or “arbitrary disqualifications” that Mr. Ramesh alleged took place in different states in astronomical numbers under the SIR process

title: Over 20 lakh medical aspirants appear for NEET-UG 2026 re-exam amidst heightened security
content: The examination is being held from 2.00 p.m. to 5.15 p.m., with the provision of an additional 15 minutes this time

title: Mysuru records over 1.21 lakh trees in citizen-led census
content: A public data portal is being planned to enable residents and others access the tree database, search for trees by ward or species and report concerns relating to tree health. The database will eventually be handed over to the Mysuru City Corporation as a living record of the city’s green infrastructure.

title: VCK urges Vijay to call for all-party meeting on Mekedatu issue
content: This comes after the CPI(M), AIADMK, and the PMK criticised the resolution passed in the Assembly on the grounds that it was not adopted in accordance with the procedure

title: ‘Operation Tiger’ successful, declares Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde
content: “Those who know me, know that we do not leave any operation incomplete,” says Shinde; all six MPs of Shiv Sena UBT are slated to merge with Shiv Sena, say party sources

title: Gehlot questions Shah’s intent in demolition of Muslim religious sites along Rajasthan border
content: Former CM alleges demolitions in border areas after Amit Shah’s Bikaner visit; says action ‘aimed at political polarisation’, urges MHA to clarify

title: India seeks to become a manufacturer in the defence sector, says PM Modi
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi says time has come for India to enter the next phase of maritime development, and the government has adopted a new vision for the shipbuilding sector

title: Tamil Nadu ammonia gas leak updates: Two women killed in gas leak in Tiruvallur seafood factory
content: Tiruvallur District Collector S. Kavitha visited the factory site and met with patients at the hospitals to oversee relief measures.

title: Vijay forms three-member committee to probe ammonia leak at seafood processing unit in Tiruvallur district
content: It will comprise the Director of Industrial Safety and Health, Member-Secretary of the TNPCB, and Additional Director of Public Health. The panel will submit an interim report within 24 hours and a final report in 3 days. Vijay also orders inspection of hazardous industrial units across the State

title: Two dead, over 60 hospitalised after ammonia leak at seafood processing unit in Tiruvallur district
content: CM announces three-member inquiry committee, ₹2 lakh for kin of the dead, both migrants from Odisha; company’s owner, manager arrested; case pending against them for prior industrial safety violations

title: MVA boycotts CM’s tea party ahead of Maharashtra Assembly’s monsoon session
content: The MVA criticises the government for not providing satisfactory answers on many issues, including drought, farmers’ issues; rise in atrocities against women, and drug trafficking; and deteriorating law and order in the State

title: Selvaperunthagai urges Kharge to relieve him as TNCC president
content: A close confidant of the TNCC president says the swift cutting of ties with the DMK, a long-term ally and a committed anti-BJP force, for the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhaga has left him disillusioned

title: From Shillong to seas: Armed forces celebrate International Day of Yoga across India
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh led the celebrations in Shillong, accompanied by Air Chief Marshal A.P. Singh and other air warriors; Army Chief General Upendra Dwivedi fronted the celebrations with troops in Tezpur; naval platforms and Indian Coast Guard’s ships at sea and at harbour also took part in the celebrations

title: Diplomatic community joins Yoga Day celebrations
content: This year marked the 12th International Day of Yoga with the theme ‘Yoga for Healthy Ageing’, which emphasised the importance of yoga for all ages

title: U.P. Muslim MPs urge government to refrain from targeted action on heritage structures
content: The MPs say it gave external adversaries unnecessary opportunities to speak about India’s internal matters, and cite Pakistan President’s comment on a Varanasi mosque

title: A ‘historic’ move fails to deliver results for AMMA
content: The series of events that led to the resignation of the film artistes forum’s executive committee reveals that not many wanted the regime headed by its first woman president to last long. Also, the organisation failed to resolve through talks even allegations of a serious nature

title: Halt eviction of people settled in forests under British-era system, Assam govt. urged
content: The All India Union of Forest Working People says the planned ejection of four ‘taungya’ villagers in Nagaon district’s Lutumari Longjap reserve forest is against the Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act of 2006

title: Commuters raise concern over non-Karnataka bikes operating as taxis amid regulatory vacuum
content: Bike taxis have emerged as a popular mode of transport in Bengaluru, offering commuters a cost-effective and time-saving alternative amid the city’s notorious traffic congestion

title: BJP is set to script history in Punjab, says BJP president Nabin
content: Nitin Nabin, who is on a three-day visit to Punjab, says the State is grappling with the menace of drugs and there is a need for a unified crusade against drug abuse

title: Congress vs Congress: Shashi Tharoor's Kashmir Remark Triggers Party Storm
content: Congress chief spokesperson in J&K, Ravinder Sharma, lashed out at the post, saying Tharoor should have spared time to meet the people of the Kashmir Valley

title: Tushar Mehta Reappointed As Solicitor General For 3 More Years
content: Tushar Mehta has represented the Union of India in several landmark cases involving constitutional interpretation, national security, electoral reforms, citizenship issues, taxation, digital regulation and Centre-State disputes.

title: Pressure Builds On Keir Starmer To Resign As UK Prime Minister
content: Starmer has publicly vowed to stay in office, but pressure is building as more and more Labour Party colleagues conclude that his time is up.

title: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Trump Issues Threat To Iran As Peace Talks Underway
content: US-Iran Peace Talks Live Updates: JD Vance had on Thursday cancelled his trip to Switzerland amid rising tension in Lebanon between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah.

title: India's Naval Self-Reliance Gets Boost As 3 Indigenous Warships Commissioned
content: The commissioning of the platforms not only augments the operational capabilities of the Navy but also reflects India's advancement in achieving defence self-sufficiency.

title: 1 Killed, Several Injured As Bus Overturns On Ahmedabad-Dholera Expressway
content: One passenger was killed and several others injured, some of them seriously, after a private bus overturned on the Ahmedabad-Dholera Expressway in Ahmedabad district on Sunday evening, police said.

title: Jailed Kashmir MP Engineer Rashid Wants To Step Down, Party To Take A Call
content: His party, the Awami Ittehad Party (AIP) said it would seek opinion of its cadres and a decision will be taken at a two-day consultative meeting of the party.

title: Bus Services To Resume In Mumbai As BEST Employees' Unions Call Off Strike
content: The indefinite strike by employees of the Brihanmumbai Electric Supply and Transport (BEST) undertaking was called off on Sunday night following a meeting between their unions and Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde, officials said.

title: "Faced Such Crisis Before": Uddhav Thackeray As Party Rebels Move To Delhi
content: The six MPs are coming to Delhi, where they will officially join the Shinde faction and hold a press conference on Monday, sources said.

title: UP Students Miss NEET Exam Due To Wrong Location Displayed On Google Maps
content: A wrong location displayed on Google Maps caused difficulties for some NEET aspirants in Uttar Pradesh's Moradabad on Sunday as they could not reach their examination centres on time and missed the medical entrance test.

title: Analysis: Bhagwant Mann's Direct Connect Push To Offset Governance Challenges
content: At the centre of this strategy is the Mann Milni programme, a public interaction initiative that has become one of the chief minister's most visible political tools.

title: Jordan Hangs 6 Over "Terrorism", Ending 9-Year Death Penalty Pause
content: A government spokesperson said more than 100 people remain on death row in Jordan and that executions would proceed "one by one".

title: "Failed Badly": Trump Says UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Will Resign
content: Trump's post came as British media reported that the embattled Labour Party leader will resign in a matter of days.

title: Nihang Group Breaks Into Uttarakhand Gurdwara, Holds Sikh Devotee Hostage On Roof
content: A heavy police force was deployed at the scene following the standoff that began Saturday evening, and efforts were underway to persuade the six Nihangs holed up in the gurdwara to come down.

title: Soldiers Free To Act To 'Eliminate Threats In Lebanon': Israel
content: Israel said the strikes were a response to projectiles fired by the Iran-backed group at its troops in southern Lebanon, prompting attacks on what an Israeli official described as "Hezbollah targets".

title: King Charles To Release Personal Tax Bill, First British Monarch To Do So
content: British monarchs are legally exempt from paying certain taxes, though they have paid some duties voluntarily for decades.

title: "Turn Over A New Leaf": US, Iran Begin Negotiating Peace In Switzerland
content: JD Vance said the goal of the talks was to "turn over a new leaf to transform our relationship with the people of Iran, and to extend an outstretched hand".

title: India plans to showcase clean energy at Brics meet
content: the 11th energy ministers' meeting will take place in Gurugram on June 25-26 . the meeting comes at a time when the world is grappling with the challenge of meeting rising energy demand while ensuring affordability, supply security and climate commitments .

title: Right way to do it: PM Modi steps off yoga dais to guide participants
content: around 35,000 participants, including school students and residents from districts, joined the PM-led event, which started at 6am and continued till around 8am on Sunday . around 30,000 other yoga enthusiasts, who failed to make it to the venue, performed asanas in the vicinity, especially on Mayo Road .

title: Don't use '100%' on food labels loosely: CCPA cracks down on misleading F&B claims
content: products contain water, fruit concentrates, preservatives, or only 87% whole wheat flour . can food and beverage companies advertise their products as "100%" natural? can they advertise products as '100% Juice' or "natural Tender Coconut Water"

title: Tamil Nadu: 2 dead, 62 ill after ammonia leak at firm; NDRF says no spread to residential areas
content: at least two people were killed and 62 others fell ill after an ammonia leak . the death toll is expected to go up as several of those in hospital are on ventilators . leak occurred at a seafood export firm in Kannigaipair .

title: 'Will take over strait, blow them up': Trump loses cool after Iran renews threat to shut Hormuz
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. title: 'Functional urban settlements': Govt may create new category
content: nearly 84% of india's population resided in urban settlements in 2025, much higher than govt's estimate of 36% . a visit to some villages close to urban areas in Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, Kerala or Madhya Pradesh will show that these have most ‘urban’ features .

title: Nishant tipped for bigger JD(U) role as Nitish gets fifth stint as party chief
content: the national council ratified Nitish Kumar’s re-election as national president for the fifth time . the party functionaries endorsed Nishant for a larger role in the organisation . JD(U) national working president says he will emerge as the brightest star on the horizon .
